title: centaurea × iasii
intro: Centaurea × iasii, also known as Iasi cornflower, is a hybrid plant of the Centaurea genus, typically characterized by its showy purple flowers and a mix of characteristics from its parent species. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, with its medicinal properties attributed to the presence of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils, which are believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The health benefits associated with Centaurea × iasii include its potential to reduce inflammation, alleviate respiratory issues, and exhibit antimicrobial and antifungal activities. Herbal preparations of Centaurea × iasii, such as infusions and decoctions, have been used in traditional medicine to treat various conditions, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
